info.title: deepseek-v4-flash
info.description:
DeepSeek Chat Completions documentation. The unified API endpoint is `/v1/chat/completions`, compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions request format.

Supported model: `deepseek-v4-flash`.

This document covers the following request fields: `model`, `messages`, `messages[].content`, `messages[].tool_calls`, `messages[].reasoning_content`, `thinking`, `stream`, `stream_options.include_usage`, `max_tokens`, `max_completion_tokens`, `stop`, `reasoning_effort`, `response_format`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `logprobs`, `top_logprobs`, `tools`, `parallel_tool_calls`, `tool_choice`, `user_id`.

tags.name: DeepSeek Chat
tags.description: DeepSeek text chat, multimodal understanding, and tool calling capabilities

post.description:
Invoke DeepSeek chat completions capability.

Supported models include `deepseek-v4-flash`.

Documented fields: `model`, `messages`, `thinking`, `stream`, `stream_options.include_usage`, `max_tokens`, `max_completion_tokens`, `stop`, `reasoning_effort`, `response_format`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `logprobs`, `top_logprobs`, `tools`, `parallel_tool_calls`, `tool_choice`, `user_id`. `messages[].content` supports `text`, `image_url`, and `video_url` multimodal parts; `assistant` messages support `reasoning_content` and `tool_calls`.

200.description: Success. Returns JSON in non-streaming mode; returns an SSE event stream in streaming mode.

message.content: The core RAG workflow typically consists of three steps: retrieval, augmentation, and generation. The system first converts the user query into a vector and retrieves relevant knowledge snippets, then concatenates the retrieved results with the original query into the prompt. Finally, the model generates an answer based on the augmented context, reducing hallucinations and improving factual accuracy.

schema.description: SSE data stream. Each `data:` event can be parsed as a DeepSeek/OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions Chunk; the stream typically ends with `data: [DONE]`. When `stream_options.include_usage=true` is set, an additional chunk with `usage` is returned before the end.

401.description: Authentication failed, e.g. missing or invalid token.

429.description: Rate limit triggered or insufficient account quota.

error.message: Insufficient account quota, please retry later

500.description: Internal server error while processing the request.

error.message: Internal processing failed, please retry later

BearerAuth.description: Pass `Authorization: Bearer <token>` in the request header.

model.description: The ID of the model to use. Supports `deepseek-v4-flash`.

messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far.

stream.description: If set to true, the model will send message deltas as SSE (server-sent events). The message stream ends with `data: [DONE]`.

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens the model can generate for the completion in a single request. The total length of input and output tokens is limited by the model's context length.

max_completion_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens the model can generate in a single request (including response and chain-of-thought). When set, `max_tokens` is ignored.

stop.description: A string or a list of up to 16 strings. The API will stop generating further tokens when it encounters any of these strings.

reasoning_effort.description: Controls the reasoning effort of the model. For normal requests, the default is high. For some complex agent-style requests (e.g. Claude Code, OpenCode), it is automatically set to max. For compatibility, low and medium are mapped to high, and xhigh is mapped to max.

frequency_penalty.description: This parameter is no longer supported. Passing it will have no effect.

presence_penalty.description: This parameter is no longer supported. Passing it will have no effect.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both.

top_p.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

logprobs.description: Whether to return the log probabilities of the output tokens. If true, the log probabilities of each output token are returned in the content of the message.

top_logprobs.description: An integer N between 0 and 20 that specifies the top N tokens by probability to return at each output position, along with their log probabilities. When this parameter is specified, logprobs must be true.

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call. Currently, only function is supported as a tool. Use this parameter to provide a list of functions that take JSON as input. Up to 128 functions are supported.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to allow the model to issue multiple tool calls in parallel.

user_id.description: Your custom user_id, with the character set [a-zA-Z0-9\-_] and a maximum length of 512. Do not include private user information in user_id. user_id can be used to distinguish users on your side for content safety handling, KVCache isolation, and scheduling isolation.

Message.description: A single message in the conversation. Depending on the role, it is divided into four message types: system, user, assistant, and tool.

SystemMessage.title: System message
SystemMessage.description: System message. The role of this message is system.

content.description: The content of the system message.

role.description: The role of the message author, which is system.

name.description: An optional name for the participant, provided to the model to distinguish between participants of the same role.

UserMessage.title: User message
UserMessage.description: User message. The role of this message is user.

content.description: The content of the user message. Can be a plain text string or an array of multimodal parts composed of `text`, `image_url`, and `video_url`.

role.description: The role of the message author, which is user.

name.description: An optional name for the participant, provided to the model to distinguish between participants of the same role.

AssistantRequestMessage.title: Assistant message
AssistantRequestMessage.description: Assistant message. The role of this message is assistant.

content.description: The content of the assistant message.

role.description: The role of the message author, which is assistant.

name.description: An optional name for the participant, provided to the model to distinguish between participants of the same role.

prefix.description: (Beta) Set this to true to force the model to start its response with the prefix content provided in this assistant message. You must set the base_url to the beta endpoint to use this feature.

reasoning_content.description: (Beta) Used in thinking mode under conversation prefix continuation, as input for the last assistant chain-of-thought content. When using this feature, the prefix parameter must be set to true.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls generated by the model, e.g. function calls.

ToolMessage.title: Tool message
ToolMessage.description: Tool message. The role of this message is tool.

role.description: The role of the message author, which is tool.

content.description: The content of the tool message.

tool_call_id.description: The ID of the tool call that this message is responding to.

ImageURL.description: Image content. Supports image URLs or Base64-encoded data.

VideoURL.description: Video content. Supports video URLs or Base64-encoded data.

ThinkingConfig.description: Controls the switch between thinking mode and non-thinking mode.

type.description: If set to enabled, thinking mode is used. If set to disabled, non-thinking mode is used.

StreamOptions.description: Options for streaming output. This parameter can only be set when the stream parameter is true.

include_usage.description: If set to true, an additional chunk will be transmitted just before the final `data: [DONE]` in the stream. The usage field on this chunk shows the token usage statistics for the entire request, while the choices field will always be an empty array. All other chunks will also include a usage field, but its value will be null.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently only function is supported.

name.description: The name of the function to call. Must consist of a-z, A-Z, 0-9 characters, or include underscores and hyphens, with a maximum length of 64 characters.

description.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to understand when and how to call it.

parameters.description: The input parameters of the function, described as a JSON Schema object.

strict.description: If set to true, the API will use strict mode in function calls to ensure the output always conforms to the function's JSON schema definition. This is a Beta feature.

ToolChoice.description: Controls the model's tool calling behavior. none means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message. auto means the model can choose to generate a message or call one or more tools. required means the model must call one or more tools. Specifying a particular tool via {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}} forces the model to call that tool.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

ResponseFormat.description: An object specifying the format the model must output.

type.description: Target output format. Set to `json_object` to enable JSON mode. When using JSON mode, you must also instruct the model to produce JSON via a system or user message.

strict.description: Whether to strictly follow the schema.

ToolCallMessage.description: Tool call object within a message.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently only function is supported.

name.description: The name of the function the model called.

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, generated by the model as a JSON string. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined in your function schema. Validate these arguments in your code before calling the function.

id.description: A unique identifier for the chat.

object.description: The type of the object, which is chat.completion.

created.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the chat completion was created.

model.description: The name of the model that generated the completion.

system_fingerprint.description: This fingerprint represents the backend configuration that the model runs with.

choices.description: A list of completions generated by the model.

index.description: The index of this completion in the list of completions generated by the model.

finish_reason.description: The reason the model stopped generating tokens. `stop` means the model finished generating naturally or hit a stop sequence; `length` means the output length reached the model's context length limit or the max_tokens limit; `content_filter` means the output was filtered due to a filtering policy; `tool_calls` means the model called a tool; `insufficient_system_resource` means generation was interrupted due to insufficient system inference resources.

role.description: The role that generated this message.

reasoning_content.description: Only applicable in thinking mode. The reasoning content in the assistant message, preceding the final answer.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls generated by the model, e.g. function calls.

Usage.description: Usage information for this chat completion request.

prompt_tokens.description: The number of tokens contained in the user prompt. This value equals prompt_cache_hit_tokens + prompt_cache_miss_tokens.

completion_tokens.description: The number of tokens generated by the model for the completion.

total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens in this request (prompt + completion).

prompt_cache_hit_tokens.description: The number of tokens in the user prompt that hit the context cache.

prompt_cache_miss_tokens.description: The number of tokens in the user prompt that missed the context cache.

reasoning_tokens.description: The number of chain-of-thought tokens generated by the reasoning model.
